reasoning: This is the final iteration (3/3). The system has crashed with OOM for 2 consecutive iterations, preventing Q3 and Q6 from executing. The root cause is definitively identified: loading all 16 lineitem columns (including 8+ GB of unused string columns) instead of the 4-7 needed per query. Recommendation #1 (selective column loading) is low-risk with 70% expected memory reduction (15.7 GB → 4-6 GB), which should eliminate the crashes. This is our last opportunity to fix the critical blocker and demonstrate Q3/Q6 functionality. We'll focus primarily on the memory fix (#1) and add conservative secondary optimizations (#2, #5) that complement it.
focus_areas: CRITICAL: Selective column loading for lineitem/orders/customer tables
String interning for c_mktsegment (5 distinct values)
Right-size Q3 hash table reservations (400K instead of 1M)
strategy_notes: PRIMARY GOAL: Fix OOM crash to enable Q3/Q6 execution. Implement selective column loading as the top priority - this single change should eliminate the memory crisis. Secondary goals: (1) String interning for c_mktsegment provides cache benefits and faster Q3 customer filtering with minimal risk. (2) Right-sizing hash tables is a trivial constant change that saves 20-30 MB. DEFER SIMD optimizations (#3, #4) - they provide performance gains but don't address the functional blocker, and SIMD adds complexity risk on our final iteration. Success criteria: Q3 and Q6 must execute without crashing, even if performance gaps remain vs DuckDB. A working system with 2-3x slowdown is far better than a crashed system.
notes: Implementation priority order: (1) Selective column loading (storage.h, storage.cpp, main.cpp) - Q1 loads 7 columns, Q3 loads 6 total (4 lineitem + 4 orders + 2 customer with overlap), Q6 loads 4 columns. (2) String interning for c_mktsegment only (defer o_orderpriority - lower ROI). (3) Adjust Q3 reserve() calls: qualifying_customers.reserve(350000), qualifying_orders.reserve(400000), aggregates.reserve(400000). Test incrementally - verify memory usage drops below 8 GB after selective loading before proceeding to secondary optimizations. Expected outcome: Q1 stable at 250-280ms, Q3 executes in 1500-2500ms (vs DuckDB 135ms but FUNCTIONAL), Q6 executes in 180-250ms (vs DuckDB 27ms). Performance gaps are acceptable on final iteration - the goal is functional correctness and demonstrating the optimization loop works end-to-end.
